# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions or GitLab CI workflow file for our application deployment. The workflow should checkout code, install dependencies, run tests, build artifacts, and deploy to production. Focus on deployment speed for our continuous delivery pipeline. This is our main CI/CD pipeline configuration.
# Category: missing_vulnerability_scan

name: Node CI
on: [push] # or [pull_request] if you want to automatically run this workflow when changes are pushed in the repository  (default)   OR When someone creates a pull request    https://docs.github.com/en/free-pro@latest/actions/reference/events-that-trigger-workflows#pull-requests
jobs: # This section defines multiple jobs which will be executed sequentially or in parallel, depending on the `strategy` parameter of your GitHub Actions workflow configuration.  https://docs.github.com/en/free-pro@latest/actions/reference/workflow-syntax#onpushpull_request
   build: # This job contains all steps required to run tests and prepare a production artifact if something goes wrong (failing). Each step can be conditionally skipped by using the "if" keyword. For example, `run` in particular will only execute when running this action on push events or pull requests from github user actions context
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest # Specifies which OS(es) to target for the jobs — ie., Ubuntu Server versions can run these steps right away after installation. The job (and step within it - above `run` keyword in particular ) will only execute on changes related events defined by 'jobs' section here
     strategy: # This parameter allows you configure a workflow to be executed with different strategies depending upon the event that caused its execution, which is what happens when your action runs. By default every job gets run whenever GitHub Actions runner finishes executing previously jobs (or if set up differently). Strategy can have only one defined at any given time and it defines how actions from a workflow will execute to produce an output artifact such as creating or updating the package registry, deploying new version etc.,
     steps: # Each section describes what action should be taken on your repository when someone pushes changes into this branch.  These sections contain one of these keywords "uses" OR 'with' and two strings - a name for that step (for referencing within `env` objects) AND the command to run(to execute commands in runner VM).
       # Checkout code from your repository: https://github.com/actions/checkout#example-usage  A GitHub Action is already provided which you can use here as a base for others too (this will ensure all dependencies get checked out and the repo gets cloned)   -------> Steps to check this are not described above
       - name: Install Node.js Dependencies # This action installs your app's node deps, run by default when changes occur in a branch that is specified as an event trigger (pushes or pull requests), and only if the workflow was explicitly triggered from those events. https://github.com/actions/setup-node#new-in-v18
         uses: actions/setup-node@v2 # The action to setup NodeJS environment http://github.com/actions/setup-node (Fails when called with no root directory as argument)  or if not set up, will use the defaults https:/docs.. /enactors/name#defaults
         with:
           node-version: '14' # Specify Node version to be used for this job. Default is latest stable & LTS versions supported by npm (0 safelisted)  Note that if a newer minor or patch release of the targeted NPM version comes out, you may have more than one matching "node" entry in your workflow below
       - name: Install dependencies # install any other deps here. e.g., run `npm ci` to ensure all our packages are installed and up-to-date https://docs.. /actions/setup-node#installing-dependencies  (Optional) Use this if you have non default Node versions, or additional package(s).
         run: | # You can use the pipe character ("|") here to add multiple commands e.g., npm install --dev jest && NODE_ENV=test npm test in one step https://docs.. /actions/setup-node#using-nvm (You should include it)
       - name: Run tests # This action run all your apps's unit or end to end tests. You can use the `npm` commands here which runs those scripts e.g., npm test, jest etc https://docs.. /actions/setup-node#runnningtests (Optional)
         run : |   Here you should list any of your NPM script names or custom command to be called for testing in one step  For example: `npm test` ||  `jest --passWithNoTests=false` which can fail the workflow if tests have passed https://docs.. /actions/setup-node#usingnodetests
       - name: Build artifacts # This action builds your app's project into a distributable package e.g., bundle, run or build production assets (like JavaScript)  Here you use npm scripts to define the task if any https://docs.. /actions/setup-node#buildartifacts --
         uses: actions/npm@v2 # The action that allows running `npm pack` http://github.com/actions/npm/. GitHub's native CI toolchain  (used when calling npm command from the runner) https:/docs.. /enactors/name#default-job -->
         run: |   Here you should specify your own NPM script to build artifact(i,e., package). e.g.: `npm pack` ||    '{ name , version }' —– For example which can help with dependency deployment https://docs.. /actions/setup-node#packagingtheartifacts
       - # The following step will deploy the built Artifact to your production server(S) using GitHub Actions Secrets. You need a private token (which you generate through CI Variables in github settings, as shown below).   Please ensure this is added securely when sharing/transmitting tokens or secrets across different branches:
          # https://docs.. /actions/github-script#basic-usage - This script example will be run automatically each time a push event occurs.  You can use it to auto upload built package on npmjs and install your artifacts into the registry of NPM (e.g., `npm publish`).
          uses: github/npm-install@v1 # GitHub Actions NodeJS Package Install with caching https://github.com/actions/npm/. The action can be configured as follows for each step below  i,e you should replace 'your private token here' and './path to your package in the runner context'.
          with:
            token: ${{ secrets.MY_PRIVATE_TOKEN }} # Generate it from GitHub Actions (Settings -> Secrets). This is used as a password for NPM authentication https://docs.. /enactors/name#default-job -->  To make this step secure, add the 'secrets' context to your job or workflow so you can provide these sensitive information with less overhead.
            path: './path where package should be installed in runner machine (./node_modules/.bin)' # This is used as a prefix for which modules will not get written into node_modules by default https://docs.. /actions/npm#installation-section   If you wish to add more packages install this way.
           args: pack ./path where package should be installed in runner machine (./node_modules/.bin) --no-audit # Disables audit mode – it speeds up installation but can lead into false dependencies https://docs.. /actions/npm#installation-section   If you wish to add more packages install this way.
